caption: Fig. 1. Spatial distributions of the coin parameters and decay curves of the probability density |ψ|2 for the π-mode for (a) the periodic lattice and (b) the Fibonacci lattice, with enlarged views of the rightmost 28 sites. The quantity |ψ|2 is plotted on a logarithmic scale, and the dashed lines represent the slopes of the ﬁtting curves indicating exponential decay. The binary coin parameters correspond to the rotation angles θW = 0.1π and θS = 0.65π. The brackets shown at the top of each panel indicate the spatial range and the correspond- ing number of S blocks required for the probability density to reach 10−15.
description2: Spatial distributions of coin parameters and decay curves of probability density |ψ|2 are plotted for π-mode in periodic and Fibonacci lattices, with enlarged views of rightmost sites.
description3: The plot displays probability density |ψ|² versus position (x) for two lattices, with dashed lines showing exponential decay slopes.
questions: How do the spatial distributions of probability density differ between the periodic and Fibonacci lattices?
What is the general trend of the decay curves for the probability density in these lattices?

caption: Fig. 2. Spatial distributions of the generated coin parameters and the corresponding quasienergy spectrum. (a) Raw output sequence from the generative model. (b) Sequence binarized from (a) with a 0.5 threshold. Region [A] denotes the hierarchical edge heterostructure. (c) Enlarged view of the rightmost 50 sites of (b). Regions [B] and [C] indicate the topological host layer and the internal S-dense domain, respectively. (d) Quasienergy spectrum. The zero and π modes, highlighted by open square (□) and open circle (⃝), are clearly isolated from the dense bulk continuum. The total number of eigenvalues is 2N, due to the two internal coin states.
description2: The figure plots spatial distributions of generated coin parameters and their corresponding quasienergy spectrum, featuring a raw output sequence, binarized sequence, and enlarged views of specific regions.
description3: The figure contains four panels displaying a raw output sequence, a binarized sequence, an enlarged view, and a quasienergy spectrum.
questions: How do the spatial distributions of coin parameters relate to the quasienergy spectrum?
What are the key structural features observed in the generated coin parameter sequence?

caption: Fig. 3. Decay curves of the probability density |ψ|2 for the π-mode local- ized at the right boundary. The panels show the results for the (a) generated sequence, and (b) principle-guided sequence constructed manually based on the generated design. The format of the ﬁgure is identical to that in Fig. 1.
description2: The figure plots decay curves of probability density |ψ|2 for a π-mode localized at the right boundary, with separate panels showing results for generated and principle-guided sequences.
description3: Two multi-panel plots display decay curves, showing probability density versus position, for generated and principle-guided sequences.
questions: How does the probability density decay near the boundary for the two sequences?
What is the general difference in the spatial extent of the probability density?
